name: 2022-12-tigris
note: Representative High/Medium set re-derived from the published report and grounded to the clone's source lines. Function names match the source (e.g. _bridgeMint, _checkDelay, verifyPrice).

known_findings.summary: verifyPrice uses Chainlink latestAnswer() only as a ±2% sanity band and never checks updatedAt/round freshness, so a stale Chainlink price (or a node price within 2% of a stale value) is accepted as a valid mark price.
_bridgeMint is declared public with no access control, so anyone can mint GovNFTs directly instead of only the LayerZero receive path, inflating governance/reward NFT supply.

The node-signed PriceData (recovered via ECDSA) has no nonce or single-use binding; within the _validSignatureTimer window the same signature can be replayed across multiple orders/positions to pin a favorable price.

addToPosition recomputes the entry price as a margin-weighted average of the old position price and the new verified price; this protocol-specific averaging can be gamed to manipulate the effective entry/liquidation price (economic invariant, not a modeled sink).
Open-fee / referral-fee accounting in _handleOpenFees can be manipulated (self-referral, fee-multiplier interactions) to reduce or redirect fees — a protocol-specific economic accounting bug.

deposit/withdraw scale by 10**(18 - token.decimals()); a margin token with more than 18 decimals underflows the exponent (and the assumption breaks for non-18-decimal tokens), corrupting the tigAsset mint/redeem amount.

_checkDelay enforces the anti-front-run open/close delay using block.number + blockDelay; block production rate is chain-dependent and irregular (L2 sequencer), so the intended time delay is not enforced as designed.

removeMargin lets a trader withdraw margin and shift the liquidation price adversely relative to the protocol's solvency intent — a protocol-specific margin/liquidation accounting invariant, not a modeled detector class.
